content: One standing preference: when the work is tightly coupled, do not split it into fake PR-shaped chunks. I want one coherent change instead of artificial fragmentation.
Understood. I will treat that as a stable workflow preference, not just a one-session comment.
I reviewed the refactor plan. The database migration, runtime change, and docs update all depend on each other closely.
There were some local details too: I renamed one helper, removed a temporary log, and shortened a test assertion. Those are not durable context.
Yes, exactly. For this kind of tightly coupled work, optimize for coherence over splitting.
Confirmed. The durable record is the workflow preference about coherent changes for tightly coupled work.
